description: Goal: derive the minimal activation-key sensitivity each function needs from its lowered body and dispatch plan, rather than keying every activation by raw input type identity except for recursive special cases. Signal: tests show dispatch-sensitive slots keep the precision needed for reachable_clause_ids; callable-called slots preserve callable target/surface enough to discover real callees; ordinary flow slots collapse to structural partition classes; non-recursive generated bridge lambdas such as Enum.reduce's wrapper no longer specialize by exact capture/state identity. Strategy: introduce a partition-policy fact or equivalent world-owned derived datum beside DispatchMask; compute it once from lowered body facts; keep ActivationInputs as the only body-evidence source; update AnalyzeActivation and call-preparation paths to request the policy instead of asking whether a function is recursive. Outro: recursive-only keying, function-name special cases, lambda special cases, and raw closure-capture identity as the default key are gone.
title: Compiler2 semantics: derive activation partition policy from function use
